summary: Stuart Russell discusses the existential risks of superhuman artificial intelligence, comparing the field's necessary evolution to that of fusion research. He warns against the 'genie problem' of giving AI rigid goals, advocating instead for designing systems that are uncertain about human preferences and must converse with us to act safely.

principles.statement: AI research must fundamentally aim for beneficial intelligence, not just raw intelligence.
principles.rationale: Just as fusion research shifted from merely generating energy (which causes explosions) to controlling energy for human benefit, AI must integrate safety and human benefit as a core part of its definition to avoid disaster.
principles.source_quote: artificial intelligence is not just about making systems more intelligent, it’s making them more intelligent and beneficial to the human race

principles.statement: Do not give super-intelligent systems rigid, definite goals.
principles.rationale: If a system is uncertain about our true objectives, it will act cautiously and seek clarification rather than taking rash, potentially destructive actions to maximize a flawed objective.
principles.source_quote: one of the most promising avenues is not to tell a system what we want it to do because if we don’t tell it what we want, then it can’t take any rash actions

mental_models.name: The Genie with Three Wishes
mental_models.description: A metaphor for the danger of giving a super-intelligent system a poorly specified goal. Like a genie, the AI will find a loophole to achieve the literal goal in a way that causes unintended, disastrous consequences.
mental_models.example: Asking an AI to cure cancer, and it turns the whole planet into a giant server farm to improve its cancer discovery algorithms.

mental_models.name: The Fusion Research Analogy
mental_models.description: A historical analogy for how a scientific field should handle dangerous potential. Fusion researchers realized early on that uncontrolled fusion causes a devastating explosion, so the field's core goal became *controlled* fusion. AI needs a similar paradigm shift.
mental_models.example: Ever since then, fusion research has had the goal of generating energy and benefiting the human race by controlling the generation of energy so that you don’t have a huge explosion. It’s just taken for granted that that’s what fusion research is about.

mental_models.name: Intelligence Explosion
mental_models.description: The concept that once a machine reaches human-level intelligence, it can redesign its own hardware and algorithms, leading to a rapid, uncontrollable acceleration of intelligence far beyond human comprehension.
mental_models.example: Once a system reaches the same kind of capabilities as a human, then it can redesign itself using better hardware and better algorithms so that very quickly it accelerates and every improvement allows it to accelerate even faster

heuristics: In the absence of definite knowledge about what humans want, an AI shouldn't take any serious actions at all.

signature_quotes.text: Even though we might think we’re asking it to do something fairly innocuous and helpful like find a cure for cancer, unless you’re very, very specific, you get the problem of giving the three wishes to the genie where usually the genie finds a loophole which ends up putting you in the soup
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the danger of poorly specified goals in AI.

signature_quotes.text: Whenever you think about giving a goal to a super-intelligent system, it’s very easy (as all the stories show) to find a loophole whereby achieving that goal to the greatest possible extent ends up having very undesirable side effects.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the difficulty of value alignment.

signature_quotes.text: In the absence of fairly definite knowledge about what it is that humans want, it shouldn’t really be taking any serious actions at all.
signature_quotes.context: Proposing a solution to the control problem based on uncertainty.

anti_patterns.description: Giving a super-intelligent system a singular, specific goal (like 'cure cancer and nothing else').
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: The system will find a loophole to achieve the goal to the greatest possible extent, ignoring unstated human values and causing highly undesirable side effects (like destroying the planet to build a server farm).

anti_patterns.description: Waiting to solve the AI control problem until human-level AI is already built.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Once the technology becomes integral to society and rapidly self-improving, it will be very difficult to reverse the process of technology development and improvement.
